sections.paragraph:
fut. יִצְפֹּן (cogn. טָמַן)-

(1) to hide, to conceal, Exo 2:2 Jos 2:4. Part. pass. צָפוּן hidden, i.e. secret, inaccessible, Eze 7:22. Especially to guard and defend any one, Psa 27:5 83:4 , צְפוּנֵי יְהֹוָה those protected by Jehovah. Intrans. to hide oneself (or ellipt. to hide nets, snares), to lie in wait, followed by לְ Pro 1:11, 18 Psa 10:8 absol. 56:7 קרי.

(2) to lay up, to store up. Part. צְפוּנִים riches, treasures, Job 20:26 Psa 17:4 קרי. Followed by לְ to lay up for any one, Job 21:19 Psa 31:20. Job 20:26, כָּל־חשֶׁךְ טָמוּן לִצְפוּנָיו “every misfortune is laid up for his treasures.” צָפַן בְּלֵב to hide with one’s self (lay up in one’s heart), Job 10:13 צ׳ אִתֹּו id. Pro 2:1 7:1 .

(3) to restrain, Pro 27:16 followed by מִן to deny to any one, Job 17:4.

Niphal, to be hidden from any one, i.e. to be unknown to him, followed by מִן Job 24:1 Jer 16:17.

(2) to be laid up, i.e. to be destined for any one, followed by לְ Job 15:20.

Hiphil, i.q. Kal No. 1, to hide, Exo 2:3 Job 14:13 to lie in wait Psa 56:7 כתיב.

Derivatives, צָפֹון (צְפֹונִי), מַצְפֻּנִים, and pr.n. צְפֹון, and-
